questions.question: Explain the need for improving food resources.
questions.answer: Improving food resources is necessary to increase food production for the rapidly growing population. Scientific farming practices help improve crop yield and livestock productivity.

questions.question: Describe different types of crops based on their use.
questions.answer: Crops are classified as cereals, pulses, oil seeds, fruits and vegetables. Cereals provide carbohydrates, pulses provide proteins and oil seeds provide edible oils.

questions.question: Explain Kharif and Rabi crops with examples.
questions.answer: Kharif crops are grown during the rainy season such as rice and maize. Rabi crops are grown during the winter season such as wheat and gram.

questions.question: Explain crop variety improvement.
questions.answer: Crop variety improvement involves developing crop varieties with desirable characteristics such as higher yield, disease resistance and better quality.

questions.question: Explain hybridisation in crops.
questions.answer: Hybridisation is the crossing of genetically different plants to produce new varieties with improved characteristics.

questions.question: Explain nutrient management in crop production.
questions.answer: Nutrient management involves supplying essential nutrients to crops using manure and fertilizers for healthy plant growth.

questions.question: Explain the importance of manure in agriculture.
questions.answer: Manure improves soil fertility, increases water holding capacity and enhances soil structure.

questions.question: Explain fertilizers and their importance.
questions.answer: Fertilizers are chemical substances that provide nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium to plants and increase crop yield.

questions.question: Explain irrigation and its importance.
questions.answer: Irrigation is the artificial supply of water to crops which ensures proper growth and increases agricultural production.

questions.question: Explain mixed cropping.
questions.answer: Mixed cropping is the practice of growing two or more crops together on the same land to reduce risk of crop failure.

questions.question: Explain intercropping with an example.
questions.answer: Intercropping is growing two or more crops in a definite row pattern such as maize and beans.

questions.question: Explain crop rotation with an example.
questions.answer: Crop rotation is growing different crops in succession on the same field such as growing legumes after cereals.

questions.question: Explain weeds and their harmful effects.
questions.answer: Weeds are unwanted plants that compete with crops for nutrients, water and sunlight and reduce crop yield.

questions.question: Explain methods of weed control.
questions.answer: Weeds can be controlled manually, mechanically or by using chemicals called herbicides.

questions.question: Explain animal husbandry.
questions.answer: Animal husbandry is the scientific management of livestock including feeding, breeding and disease control.

questions.question: Explain cattle farming.
questions.answer: Cattle farming involves rearing cattle for milk production and agricultural work.

questions.question: Explain poultry farming.
questions.answer: Poultry farming is the raising of birds such as hens and ducks for eggs and meat production.

questions.question: Explain fish production.
questions.answer: Fish production includes capture fishing from natural water bodies and culture fishing in ponds.

questions.question: Explain composite fish culture.
questions.answer: Composite fish culture is growing different fish species together in a pond to increase fish production.

questions.question: Explain aquaculture.
questions.answer: Aquaculture is the farming of fish and other aquatic organisms in water bodies.

questions.question: Explain bee-keeping.
questions.answer: Bee-keeping or apiculture is the rearing of honey bees for honey and wax production.

questions.question: Explain pasturage in apiculture.
questions.answer: Pasturage refers to the availability of flowers from which bees collect nectar and pollen.

questions.question: Explain the benefits of bee-keeping.
questions.answer: Bee-keeping provides honey, wax and improves crop pollination.

questions.question: Explain grain storage.
questions.answer: Grain storage is the process of keeping harvested grains safe from pests, insects and moisture.

questions.question: Explain mixed farming.
questions.answer: Mixed farming is the practice of growing crops and raising livestock together on the same farm.
